diagrams.title: Monoalphabetic Cipher Encryption Process
diagrams.description: Shows how plaintext letters flow through the substitution permutation to produce ciphertext
elements: Plaintext: H E L L O
Permutation Table (Key): A→X, B→Y, C→Z, D→P, ...
Process: Each letter finds its row in permutation, outputs corresponding ciphertext letter
Output: X P G G W
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the fixed one-to-one mapping nature of monoalphabetic cipher and why frequency is preserved

diagrams.title: Frequency Analysis Attack Method
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing steps in cryptanalyzing monoalphabetic cipher using frequency analysis
elements: Step 2: Compare with standard English frequency table
Step 3: Map highest ciphertext letter to highest plaintext letter (E)
Step 5: Iteratively refine mappings with context
diagrams.purpose: Visual guide to systematic frequency analysis approach for breaking monoalphabetic ciphers

diagrams.title: Keyspace Comparison: Caesar vs Monoalphabetic
diagrams.description: Comparative visualization of key distribution and attack complexity
elements: Caesar: 26 keys shown as circular rotation (shift 0-25)
Monoalphabetic: 26! keys as scrambled permutation boxes
Frequency analysis: Both broken in seconds (same vulnerability)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates why key space size alone doesn't determine cipher security
